name: Nippon India ETF Nifty 50

index.name: Nifty 50
index.description: NIFTY 50 is India's premier blue-chip equity benchmark tracking the 50 largest and most liquid companies spanning 13 sectors. Represents ~65% of NSE's free-float market capitalization, serving as barometer for India's economic health. Launched November 3, 1995 with base index of 1,000, now surpassed 25,000 milestone (25x return over 30 years = 16.5% CAGR including dividends). Free-float market cap methodology ensures larger, fundamentally stronger companies dominate allocation — Reliance (9.8%), HDFC Bank (8.5%), ICICI Bank (7.2%) control ~25% index weight. Most liquid large-cap index globally with derivatives volume exceeding ₹5 lakh crore daily (Nifty futures, options, mini contracts). Perfect core equity holding for conservative long-term investors, retirement portfolios, and index-replicating passive strategies. Nifty 50 beta=1 by definition, thus returns track both bull and bear markets. Lowest expense ratio (0.05-0.10%) for passive equity exposure. Includes Reliance's O2C + Jio bundle, HDFC-HDFC Bank combined financial behemoth, TCS/Infosys IT export powerhouse, and PSU revival (ONGC, Coal India, NTPC).

name: HDFC SENSEX ETF

index.name: BSE SENSEX
index.description: The S&P BSE SENSEX is India's oldest and most recognized index, calculated since 1986 with base year 1978–79 = 100. Comprises 30 financially sound blue-chip companies listed on BSE — Asia's oldest exchange (est. 1875). Serves as the primary barometer of the Indian equity market and is used globally as India's economic indicator. Crossed 80,000 for the first time in July 2024.

name: UTI Nifty Next 50 ETF

index.name: Nifty Next 50
index.description: Tracks companies ranked 51-100 by free-float market cap on NSE — often called the 'feeder index' because many constituents graduate to the Nifty 50. Combines large-cap stability with higher growth potential. Historically outperformed the Nifty 50 over long periods. Sector composition is more diversified than Nifty 50 with higher weight to consumption, real estate and healthcare.

name: Nippon India ETF Junior BeES

index.name: Nifty Next 50
index.description: Tracks Nifty Next 50 index — companies ranked 51-100. Often called 'the feeder to Nifty 50'. Companies that graduate from Next 50 to Nifty 50 deliver re-rating gains. Strong 10-year SIP returns historically outperform large-cap active funds.

name: SBI ETF Nifty 50

index.name: Nifty 50
index.description: SBI ETF Nifty 50 is India's single largest ETF by AUM — managing ₹2.44 lakh crore, it dwarfs every other ETF on NSE by an order of magnitude. This extraordinary scale stems from government mandate: EPFO (Employees' Provident Fund Organisation) channels 15% of incremental corpus into this ETF, making it the vehicle for India's retirement savings entering equity markets. Every Indian formal-sector employee's PF contribution partly flows into SETFNIF50. The ETF tracks Nifty 50 identically to NIFTYBEES and UTINIFTETF, but its size creates unique market dynamics — large block deals during EPFO rebalancing move the entire market. Expense ratio at 0.07% is among the lowest available. Unlike retail-facing ETFs, the majority of SETFNIF50 units are held institutionally (EPFO, insurance companies, government pension funds), giving it the lowest daily trading volume relative to AUM. Retail investors can buy it freely on NSE at minimal cost, enjoying the same Nifty 50 exposure as government pension funds. SBI Mutual Fund is India's largest AMC, providing institutional-grade reliability and zero AMC failure risk.
